response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced generalist engineer with over two decades of breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, but the core technical requirements of this role — deep Java/Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and experience with enterprise frameworks like Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate — are almost entirely absent from the resume. His primary stack is TypeScript and Node.js, Java appears only briefly in a 2009–2011 context with no modern JVM work, and there is no evidence of Spring ecosystem experience or IDE internals work beyond a C-based IDE tooling role at National Instruments in 2007–2008. The role demands a specialist; this profile is a generalist.
strengths: 20+ years of broad engineering experience across complex, high-throughput systems (e.g., Sociomantic real-time ad infrastructure handling millions of requests/second)
Strong software craftsmanship foundation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and clean architecture applied consistently across roles
Prior exposure to developer tooling and IDE-adjacent work (NI LabWindows/CVI contributions, MCP code generators, open-source test frameworks)
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin experience — primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js with Java listed only as a peripheral skill last used substantively around 2009–2011
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any Java enterprise framework experience anywhere in the resume
No JVM internals knowledge demonstrated; IDE work at National Instruments was C/C++-based and predates the role's technical domain entirely

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework exposure
Strong craftsmanship and systems complexity experience
